headlines.title: China’s Oil Imports Plummet to Eight-Year Low
headlines.summary: Crude oil imports to China in May fell to their lowest since October 2017 because of the price spike resulting from the Persian Gulf tanker traffic disruption. The May total stood at 33 million barrels, or 7.8 million barrels daily, Bloomberg reported, citing Chinese customs data

headlines.title: More LNG Tankers Slip Through the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Another LNG carrier loaded with Qatari gas has passed through the Strait of Hormuz this week, bringing the total number of Qatari LNG cargoes that have cleared the chokepoint since the start of the war to five, Reuters reported today, citing data from Kpler and LSEG. headlines.title: How Fake News Could Send Oil Prices Soaring
headlines.summary: Oil has been surging since the beginning of the Iran war, taking barrels off the market—but the market has no way of knowing, in real time, whether the next disruption it reacts to actually happened because energy prices don’t wait for confirmation. headlines.summary: Oil prices began this week with a jump following the latest flare-up of hostilities in the Middle East, this time between Iran and Israel. This is worse news for the global economy than it would have been a month ago. The world’s oil reserves are starting to run dry, and nothing

headlines.title: Airfares Are Set to Rise Again as the Fuel Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: Prices for flights will shoot up again if fuel costs stay high, the chief of British Airways has warned.
